introduction:
The Ration Card in Delhi is issued by the Department of Food, Supplies and Consumer Affairs, Government of NCT of Delhi. It aims to ensure food security for eligible residents by providing subsidised food grains (wheat, rice, sugar) through Fair Price Shops (FPS).

In Delhi, rationing is predominantly online and Aadhaar-driven under the National Food Security Act (NFSA). Note: The eldest female member (above 18 years) of the family must be designated as the Head of the Family on the ration card.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost
  • Processing time is usually within 30-45 working days
  • Eldest female member MUST be the Head of Household
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
  • Applications are primarily accepted online through the e-District Delhi portal
  • Helpline: 1967 or 1800-11-0841 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Keep digital copies ready for upload if applying online. Present originals if visiting the Circle Office for verification.
Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card of all members (Mandatory)
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
Address Proof (for Delhi)
  • Aadhaar Card (matching Delhi address)
  • Electricity / Water Bill (recent)
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Bank Passbook (with photo and address)
  • House Allotment letters (if applicable)
Income & Family Documents
  • Income Certificate (issued by SDM/Tehsildar in Delhi)
  • Passport-size photo of the female Head of Family
  • Birth Certificates (for members below 5 years)
  • Caste Certificate (if applying under SC/ST/OBC quota)
Category Guide
  • PR (Priority Household): Basic NFSA category for families below specific income limits
  • AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana): For the poorest of the poor families
  • JRC (Jhuggi Ration Card): For residents of slum areas

Note: Current caps on total beneficiaries may affect immediate PR issuance.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-District Delhi Portal

Delhi Government highly recommends applying online through the e-District Delhi portal. You must have an Aadhaar-linked mobile number to proceed.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit e-District Portal
    Go to edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in
  2. Register as New User
    Click on "New User" in the Citizen's Corner. Register using the Aadhaar Number of the eldest female member of your family. Set a password.
  3. Login and Select Service
    Log in with your User ID and Password. Go to the "Apply for Services" section and click on "Issuance of New Ration Card" under the Dept of Food and Supply.
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter details for the Head of Family and add all family members. You'll need their Aadhaar numbers. Provide your complete Delhi address and select your desired Fair Price Shop (FPS).
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of Aadhaar cards, Income Certificate, Address proof, and photograph of the Head of Family. Ensure files are within the specified size limits (usually under 100KB per file).
  6. Submit & Acknowledge
    Submit the application. You will receive an Application Number. Print the acknowledgement slip for future reference.
  7. Verification (FSO)
    A Food Supply Officer (FSO) or an Inspector from the Food Department will verify your application. A physical verification visit to your address may be conducted.
Tip: Ensure your registered mobile number is always active to receive status updates. You can track your application online.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply via Circle Office / CSC (Assisted Mode)

If you cannot apply online yourself, you can visit a nearby Common Service Centre (CSC) or the local Food & Supply Circle Office for assistance.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate Circle Office / CSC
    Find your respective Circle Office addressing your locality on the NFS portal.
  2. Visit providing required documents
    Take the Head of Family (eldest female) along. Working hours are generally Monday \u2013 Friday, 10:00 AM \u2013 4:00 PM.
  3. Submit Application Details
    A CSC or office operator will fill the application on the e-portal on your behalf using your details.
  4. Biometric/Document Scan
    Provide original documents for scanning. They will be uploaded instantly.
  5. Collect Receipt
    Take the computer-generated Acknowledgement Receipt. Pay any nominal CSC convenience fee (usually ₹30-₹50) if applicable.
  6. Verification
    Wait for the area Inspector to verify the address and details physically within a few weeks.
  7. Card Issuance
    If approved, your e-Ration card will be generated. You can download it directly from the portal. physical cards may also be dispatched.
Processing Time: Typically 30-45 working days, subject to the availability of new quotas under NFSA in Delhi.
afterApplying:
After Applying \u2014 What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit the NFS E-PDS Portal or e-District portal.
Click "Track Food Security Application" and enter your Aadhaar or Application Number.

Download e-Ration Card

Delhi provides digital e-Ration Cards. Once approved, you can download the e-Ration Card from the NFS portal using your Aadhaar or Ration Card number.

Check Fair Price Shop (FPS)

You can locate your mapped FPS dealer and their stock details on the NFS portal under "Know Your FPS Dealer".

Grievance Redressal

If you face any issues, you can call the state helpline 1967 or file a grievance through the PGMS portal.
